sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 1.1g. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 1.1g. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 60.1g. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 60.1g. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 25.8g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 25.8g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin e at 3% DV, vitamin c at 8% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin e at 3% DV, vitamin c at 8% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: iron at 37% DV, magnesium at 30% DV, zinc at 23% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: iron at 37% DV, magnesium at 30% DV, zinc at 23% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: one of the best budget staple foods because it is unusually cheap, filling, and useful in real meals compared with a lot of healthy-food staples. very filling for the calories because the fibre and protein combo gives it strong practical fullness. usually affordable and practical because makes it easy to use as a real staple rather than a niche health food.

sections.subtitleText: on the plus side: one of the best budget staple foods because it is unusually cheap, filling, and useful in real meals compared with a lot of healthy-food staples. very filling for the calories because the fibre and protein combo gives it strong practical fullness. usually affordable and practical because makes it easy to use as a real staple rather than a niche health food.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: contains some anti-nutrients because phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat. can be rough on some people digestively because not everyone handles legumes equally well. needs some cooking effort because a small practical friction versus more convenient foods.

closing.summary: Lentils has a genuinely strong overall case, especially one of the best budget staple foods and very filling for the calories. the downsides are there, but contains some anti-nutrients and can be rough on some people digestively do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
